The results showed that the percentage of all the aspects in usability from the redesigned version were all higher than the current one with 80% of effectiveness, 80% of efficiency, 80% of usefulness, 86.67% of satisfaction, and 73.33% of learnability. Therefore, based on the research results, the redesign of Go Siaga is more effective, more efficient, more useful, more satisfying, and also easy to learn.
Judul Paper: Implementation of Design Thinking Method in UI/UX Redesign of Public Complaint Application (Case Study: Go Siaga App)
Keyword: Technology; Informatics Engineering; Computer Science
Jurnal: TEKNIK INFORMATIKA

Abstrak: Incident Management in ITIL requires an effective process so the incidents do not disrupt business processes for too long. This research aims to automate decision-making in Incident Management process. To perform the automation in decision-making process requires machine learning algorithms. The development of machine learning method in this research will bring significance result such as a new technique of decision-making process in Incident Management, accelerate decision-making process in Incident Management by implementing machine learning to determine the category, group, and priority. By combining supervised and unsupervised machine learning, this research can help to determine the priority of the incident, so IT Operation Teams know which incident should resolve first. By training historical full description, short description, and title, machine learning can classify the new incident. In this research different classification algorithms are used to automate decision making process. Performances of automated decision-making are evaluated with accuracy, precision, recall, and f1-score. Based on the result of various performance metrics, classifier based on K-Nearest Neighbor performed well on predicting Priority, and both category and priority get the best performance with Support Vector Machine.
Judul Paper: Enhancing ITIL Incident Management: Innovative Machine Learning Approaches for Efficient Incident Prioritization and Resolution
Keyword: IT Service Management, IT Infrastructure Library, Incident Management, Machine Learning
Jurnal: TEKNIK INFORMATIKA

Abstrak: Indonesia is one of the countries with the highest number of social media users. Million social media users in Indonesia reached 167 million in January 2023. These users are spread, across various social media, including Twitter with 24 million users. The high number of social media users on Twitter makes the information validation process even more neglected. Moreover, the trend of news interest read by social media users is only adjusted to their individual tastes. This phenomenon is evidenced by the large number of fake news (hoaxes) circulating in society which are spread through social media. Therefore, an accurate machine learning model is needed to classify "real" and "hoax" news. This study uses the TfidfVectorizer algorithm and Passive Aggressive Classifier for datasets that are shared through the Kaggle site. The contents of the dataset were sourced via social media Twitter over a span of 5 years, namely 2015-2020. At the preprocessing stage to making the Confusion Matrix, the machine learning model shows that it can work well as expected, namely getting Accuracy, Precision, and Recall scores of 82.44%, 80.66%, and 82.44%. In addition, the results of the confusion matrix show that in the dataset used, there is more "real" news than "hoaxes", that is, the model is able to predict 1059 real news and 211 hoax news, with actual conditions 1106 real news and 164 hoax news.
Judul Paper: Hoax News Detection Using Passive Aggressive Classifier And TfidfVectorizer
Keyword: Machine Learning; Hoax News; Social Media; Classification; TfidfVectorizer; Passive Aggressive Classifier
Jurnal: TEKNIK INFORMATIKA

Abstrak: Diagnosis is an activity that refers to the examination of something. Diagnosis is often associated with medical activities as a determinant of a person's condition, in the health sector diagnosis means a procedure performed by a doctor to determine a patient's condition. Unfortunately, it is rare to diagnose disease using an object wound, whereas if the wound is not treated immediately it can lead to more serious illnesses such as ulcers and tetanus or in some cases it can cause infection which then becomes a complication, in the worst case amputation occurs. The skin protects the body from various threats, the skin is also the first fortress for the body. Before implementing a prototype external wound diagnosis, it is necessary to test the accuracy of the algorithm to be used. The algorithm that can be used for diagnosis or classification is the Support Vector Machine or SVM which in the process goes through 3 stages, namely data collection, preprocessing, and classification. This research obtained the results of feature extraction on the wound image test data using GLCM with a contrast value of 0.0082, a correlation value of 0.9769, an energy value of 0.6391, and a homogeneity value of 0.9959 as well as the accuracy of using the SVM algorithm which was measured using a confusion matrix to get an accuracy value of 96.39%, 93.06% precision, recall 92.85%, and F1-score 92.58%. The results of the accuracy of the classification of external wound images using the SVM algorithm are 92.85%.
Judul Paper: Medical External Wound Image Classification Using Support Vector Machine Technique
Keyword: Support Vector Machine; External Wound; Confusion Matrix.
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: Language Identification (LID) aims to guess or identify which language the text or sound is coming from. Language identification tends to be easier in languages with different characteristics (e.g., Indonesian and English), but not for languages with similar characteristics (e.g., Indonesian and Malaysian). Similar languages can cause ambiguity that will be a bias for machine learning. Using Support Vector Machine (SVM) technique, this research tried to identify the Indonesian or Malaysian language. The training and testing data are taken from Leipzig Corpora Collection and Twitter dataset. The feature representation technique uses TF-IDF, and the baseline testing uses Naive Bayes Multinomial. We used two training techniques: split (20:80) and 10-cross validation. The experimental results show that the accuracy between the baseline and SVM is not too far. Both provide accuracy of around 90% and above. The results indicate that Indonesian and Malaysian language identification accuracy is relatively high even though using simple techniques.
Judul Paper: Automatic Language Identification for Indonesian-Malaysian Language Using Machine Learning
Keyword: Language Identification; Indonesian; Malaysian; Support Vector Machine
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: The need for food, especially rice, continues to increase. Therefore, a production increase of around 70% is required to meet the demand. In this case, supervision and care of rice from planting must be carried out efficiently, which can be done by deep learning, namely Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN). The classification was carried out on the image of rice seedlings in the form of rice seedlings and bare land patch images. The main purpose of this research is to conduct a comparison test of the performance of each CNN model with a lightweight architecture and validate the architecture. A lightweight CNN architecture is used due to its lower architecture size but still has decent performance compared to the regular CNN model for the rice seedlings dataset. Training and testing were carried out on the Rice Seedling Dataset to determine the performance of the proposed method. The research was built using the PyTorch library and the Python programming language and resulted in 99% of accuracy, precision, recall, kappa, and F-1 Score. In addition, validation was carried out using K-Fold Cross Validation which also had the best accuracy of 99%. Therefore, we conclude that the developed model can properly classify images of rice seedlings and arable land.
Judul Paper: Rice Seedling Image Classification Using Light Convolutional Neural Network
Keyword: lightweight convolution neural network; rice seedling; arable land; image classification; farming
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: The great challenge in Indonesian automatic text summarization research is producing readable summaries. The quality of text summary can be reached if the meaning of the text can be maintained properly. As a result, the purpose of this study is to improve the quality of extractive Indonesian automatic text summarization by taking into account the quality of structured text representation. This study employs Sequential Pattern Mining (SPM) to generate a sequence of words as a structured representation of text and a graph-based approach to generate automatic text summarization. The SPM algorithm used is PrefixSpan, and the graph-based approach uses the Bellman-Ford algorithm. The results of an experiment using the IndoSum dataset show that combining SPM and Bellman-Ford can improve the precision, recall, and f-measure of ROUGE-1, ROUGE-2, and ROUGE-L. When Bellman-Ford is combined with SPM, the F-measure of ROUGE-1 increases from 0.2299 to 0.3342. The ROUGE-2 f-measure increases from 0.1342 to 0.2191, and the ROUGE-L f-measure increases from 0.1904 to 0.2878. This result demonstrates that SPM can improve the performance of the Bellman-Ford algorithm in producing Indonesian text summaries.
Judul Paper: Combination of Graph-based Approach and Sequential Pattern Mining for Extractive Text Summarization with Indonesian Language
Keyword: automatic text summarization; Bellman-Ford algorithm; graph-based approach; Indonesian language; prefixspan; sequence of words; sequential pattern mining
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: Ship stability is very important for the safety of ship motion. There are many factors that affect the stability of a ship. One of the causes of accidents on ships is the problem of ship stability, including the ship cannot be controlled, and loses balance due to improper placement of cargo loads. This study combines gyroscopes, accelerometers, compasses, and GPS sensors, so that more accurate ship tilt information is obtained through an Android smartphone application. This study uses the fuzzy inference system (FIS) method with a trapezoidal membership function where there are 2 inputs and 1 output. Ship tilt input uses 3 linguistic variables very tilted, tilted, and stable. The slope duration input uses 5 very fast, fast, fairly fast, slow, and very slow linguistic variables. Ship status output is divided into 3 linguistic variables safe, alert, and dangerous. Testing and implementation with an input slope of 4.8 and a slope duration of 10 seconds using the Sugeno fuzzy method, the ship's crips value of 0.65 with an alert status was obtained. Calculation of the accuracy of the gyroscope sensor error using the MAPE method, the result is an error percentage of 6.55% (very good). The system accuracy error of 39 trials (36 correct and 3 incorrect) is 92.30% (very good). This research is expected to make it easier for the captain to monitor the stability of the ship and can provide notification of the status of the ship to the captain of the ship if there is a condition of the ship that needs to be watched out for. In addition, the notification will also be received by Port officers on land.
Judul Paper: Design Development of Detection System and Ro-Ro Ship Notification based on Fuzzy Inference System
Keyword: Smartphone, FIS, notification system, ship tilt, ship stability
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: Writing Javanese script is considered complicated and difficult for people who learn it. The process of transliterating Latin into Javanese script cannot be done directly, because each alphabet is not always represented by one Javanese script. Javanese script is not represented by one or more Latin letters, so if transliteration of Latin letters to Javanese letters is required, a parsing process is required. The rows of Javanese letters form a ligature with certain rules, so parsing is also needed to arrange the rows of Javanese letters correctly. This study aims to design a program to facilitate the transliteration of Latin script to Javanese script.  Finite State Automata (FSA) is used to describe writing rules. This study is limited to lowercase letters only, capital letters will be subtracted first, number symbols are not discussed in this study. The results of the study are in the form of a program design that can transliterate Latin writing into Javanese. Experiments were carried out as many as 4 structures of vowel consonant variations. All syllabic structures that include KV, KKV, KVK, KKVK have been tried. The transliteration results show conformity with a 100% accuracy rate in accordance with the rules of writing Javanese script. This research shows that the application of FSA can handle the transliteration of Latin letters into Javanese.
Judul Paper: Role of Finite State Automata in Transliterating Latin Script into Javanese Script
Keyword: FSA; Javanese Transliteration; Javanese Script; Parser
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: Iris is one of the reliable biometrics because it has a texture that rich properties and the texture is not changeable lifetime. Iris recognition has drawbacks in the matching process when using contact lenses. Contact lens can changes in the texture of the iris, which can reduce the accuracy of recognition. Therefore, a system is needed to detect contact lenses while someone is detected using contact lens, the system can reject the registration or authentication process. Methods used to detect contact lenses are Domain Specific Binarized Statistical Image Feature (BSIF) and Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) for feature extraction. Both methods are fused and modeled using the Support Vector Machine (SVM). Based on the test results, the most optimal kernel is 5x5 12bit. Using the kernel, the accuracy and f1 score obtained 99.1%. In the experiments conducted, this research applies Principal Component Analysis (PCA) to reduce features. However, the role of PCA does not affect the performance of the model. The best model tested with real life data, the Pocophone f1 smartphone and CCTV were used to take pictures of the eyes. The Result 6 experiments wich are 4 without contact lenses and 2 wearing contact lenses, there are only 2 detected correctly. This is because the ability of the images taken from the Poco F1 and CCTV have low resolution.
Judul Paper: Contact Lens Detection Using Domain Specific BSIF and Discrete Wavelet Transform
Keyword: contact lens detection;Domain Specific BSIF;Discrete Wavelet Transform;SVM;Computer Vision;Hancrafted;Iris
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: Medical image classification plays significant role in the process of medical decisions making, especially during the difficult period of the pandemic. One method being considered good at such classification is Convolutional Neural Network, in which we use pre-trained model approach with transfer learning since the limitation of medical images may require optimal effort. Through this pre-trained model with transfer learning, the objective is to maximize the accuracy of classification and to push forward the training session throughout the comparison of both transfer learning and non-transfer learning based pre-train models. The first type provides average accuracy of 0.84 with approximate training time 0.54 hour while the latter shows the average result of accuracy as 0.74 with average training time 0.58 hour. As the result, the optimizations are 1.13x for accuracy and 1.1x for training time. EfficientNetV2 is one pre-trained model selected for this project, being exposed to both transfer learning and non-transfer learning approach systems. The transfer learning version provides the superior accuracy as 0.88 and training time as 31 minutes - 50 seconds, showing the accuracy of 0.94 on validation and 0.88 on testing
Judul Paper: The Advantage of Transfer Learning with Pre-Trained Model in CNN Towards Ct-Scan Classification
Keyword: CT-Scan; CNN; Classification; EfficientNetV2; Transfer Learning
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: Cancer is a disease that is widely known and suffered by people in various countries. One type of cancer classified as the third contributor to death is colon cancer, with a mortality rate of 9.4%. Colon cancer is cancer that attacks the large intestine or rectum. Classification of colon cancer promptly is necessary to carry out appropriate treatment to reduce the death rate from colon cancer. This study uses the ANFIS method to classify colon cancer with its texture analysis using GLRLM. In addition, the evaluation model used in this study is the K-fold cross-validation method. This research uses colon cancer histopathological image data, which is 10000 image data divided into 2 classes, namely 5000 benign class and 5000 adenocarcinoma class. The best result in this study is when using k = 5 at an orientation angle of 135°, the accuracy value is 85.57%, sensitivity is 91.72%, and specificity is 80.55%.
Judul Paper: Classification of Colon Cancer Based on Hispathological Images using Adaptive Neuro Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS)
Keyword: ANFIS; classification; colon cancer; GLRLM; K-fold cross validation
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: A good service system will satisfy the community, making that community’s contentment the deciding factor or the key factor in determining how successful an organization is in providing the service. This research aims to analyze the service system that has been available in the Department of Population and Civil Registration of Buru South district through the public satisfaction survey as well as to understand the services system that should be improved to minimize public dissatisfaction with the procedures provided by using the machine learning model, namely Random Forest Classifier technique to obtain a prediction of the satisfaction of the public with the services provided and perform validity testing on the prediction results obtained from the Random forest classifier technique using the Technology Acceptance Model. (TAM). The results of the trials carried out there are 3 determining factors to be able to increase public dissatisfaction namely the complaint service, the service process and the behavior of the officer supported by the validity test results using TAM with the results showing that the 3 services are valid means to be a factor that can be used to increase the public satisfaction with the result obtained from the T-computed value greater than T-table with the value for the Complaint Service 4.4794, service process 2.1345 and the Officer Behavior 1.9675 of the value of the table 1.6517.
Judul Paper: Analysis of Community Satisfaction with the Service Systems in Civil Registry Service Office, South Buru Regency using the TAM (Technology Acceptance Model) Method
Keyword: Public satisfaction; Machine Learning; TAM (Technology Acceptance Model).
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: Twitter had 24 million users in Indonesia at the beginning of 2023. Despite having fewer users than other platforms, its fast and instant nature makes Twitter a significant source of information dissemination. Tweets shared on Twitter offer various advantages. However, it also has negative consequences, including the dissemination of fake news, instances of cyberbullying, and the expression of hate speech. Specifically, hate speech employs offensive language to discriminate against an individual or group based on race, ethnicity, nationality, religion, gender, sexual orientation, or other personal attributes, leading to discord. Such behavior comes under the jurisdiction of various legal statutes, including the Constitution, the Criminal Code, and the ITE Law. The primary objective of this research is to categorize tweets shared on Twitter into hate speech and non-hate speech sentiments, utilizing a Support Vector Machine (SVM) algorithm based on a dataset of 5,000 tweets. This research involved data preprocessing, labeling, feature extraction using TF-IDF, model training (80%), and testing (20%). The final stage includes enhancing SVM parameters through GridSearch and cross-validation methods (GridSearchCV), followed by analysis using a Confusion Matrix with the Matplotlib Library. Radial Basis Function (RBF) kernels, defined by parameters C=10 and gamma=0.1, exhibited the highest performance among SVM models, boasting an 84% accuracy. The RBF kernel also attained 85% precision, 97% recall, and a 91% F1-score for hate speech identification. In conclusion, the evaluation of SVM kernel performance highlights the superiority of RBF kernels in achieving the highest accuracy, complemented by nuanced insights into hate speech precision, recall, and F1-score values across various kernel types.
Judul Paper: Identifying Hate Speech in Tweets with Sentiment Analysis on Indonesian Twitter Utilizing Support Vector Machine Algorithm
Keyword: Sentiment Analysis; Hate Speech; Indonesian Twitter; Support Vector Machine Algorithm; Machine Learning
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: The majority of universities in Indonesia make maximum efforts to participate and excel in the implementation of the Student Creativity Program (namely: PKM). PKM requires students to collaborate interdisciplinary or multidisciplinary. The problem at the beginning of PKM implementation is establishing groups, where students find it difficult to have fellow students or supervisors from different disciplines in groups and according to the topic of their PKM. This research develops a user-oriented interaction and interface of information systems and group management. Development was carried out through a User-Centered Design which consisted of the following stages: plan the human-centered process; understand and specify the context of use; specify the user requirements; produce design solutions to meet user requirements; and evaluate the design against the requirements. Testing using System Usability Scale (SUS) and User Experience Questionnaire (UEQ) approach were conducted on 30 respondents who had competencies as business analysts, programmers, and testers. The results of the mean score of the SUS test are 80, and the mean Score of the UEQ test on the Attractiveness variable is 1.922, Clarity is 2.158, Efficiency is 2.042, Accuracy is 1.708, Stimulation is 1.967, and Novelty is 1.917. The system has achieved the goals and user experience because all the testing scores are above the standard provisions.
Judul Paper: Development of Information and Management System of Student Competition Groups through User-Centered Design Approach
Keyword: Student Creativity Program; User-Centered Design; System Usability Scale; User Experience Questionnaire
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: One of the arts in Surakarta culture is batik cloth. A batik is a form of heritage from the nation's ancestors whose manufacturing process must use specific tools and materials. Surakarta's typical batik has many patterns and motifs, such as Sawat, Satriomanah, and Semenrante. The pattern is a picture framework whose results will display the type of batik. A batik may resemble one type and another, so a classification technique is needed to determine the type of batik. This study aims to develop a classification method for batik cloth using the Naïve Bayes classification technique. The feature extraction used is the Gray Level Co-Occurrence Matrix (GLCM) to obtain texture values in each image. The stages in this research include pre-processing, feature extraction, classification, and testing. The training data in this study were 200 images for each Sawat, Satriomanah, and Sementrante class obtained from the data augmentation method by flipping, zooming, cropping, shifting, and changing the brightness of the images. The total sample data is 600 images. The amount of training data and data testing was divided three times (60% training and 40% testing), (70% training and 30% testing), and (80% training and 20% testing) for accuracy. In this study, the Naïve Bayes method using WEKA 3.8.6 tools obtained the best accuracy of 97.22% using a 70% percentage split compared to using 80% and 60% percentage splits with a result of 96.66%, this difference occurs due to differences in training data and test data. The results of this study indicate that the Naïve Bayes method can be used to classify batik cloth patterns based on texture feature extraction.
Judul Paper: Batik Pattern Classification using Naïve Bayes Method Based on Texture Feature Extraction
Keyword: Classification, GLCM, Naïve Bayes, Surakarta Batik Pattern
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: Financial literacy is a person's skills regarding financial knowledge and behavior. In 2019, the National Survey of Financial Literacy and Inclusion (SNLIK) stated that Indonesia had a low level of financial literacy with a percentage of 38.03%. In 2016, the Indonesian government incorporated financial literacy into the K13 curriculum for the high school level to improve financial literacy at the student level. In line with that, students need facilities to help them learn financial literacy. This study aims to build an interaction design of financial mathematics calculators using the User-Centered Design method. The limitation of this study is the design uses a mobile web platform that targets high school students. The results of usability testing on effectiveness get an average task-completion rate score of 92.3%, an average efficiency of 92.0% overall relative efficiency, user satisfaction using the System Usability Scale (SUS) gets a score of 93.2 for the usability factor and a score of 74 for the learnability factor. The findings of this study indicate that the application is easy to use and provides a positive experience in learning financial literacy. The current study's results will support this application's use in future research as a new tool for providing financial literacy.
Judul Paper: Measuring Usability on User-Centered Mobile Web Application: Case Study on Financial Mathematics Calculator
Keyword: financial literacy, financial math calculator, user-centered design, usability testing, system usability scale
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: Along with the development of the internet, the need for data security is increasing. Ensuring the security of data requires special ways. Among these are cryptography and steganography. Because of the need for this method, many new data security methods have emerged. One of them is using DNA media. DNA cryptography and steganography utilize the properties of DNA to secure and hide secret messages. This paper proposes a combined method between the Playfair cipher cryptographic technique and the DNA substitution steganography algorithm for data security. Comparative analysis was conducted on techniques that are being tested by using similar methods. Four tests are conducted to measure the performance of the proposed method and another method was tested for comparative purposes. Four tests that were conducted are hiding capacity and key length test, speed test, endurance test, and steganalysis test. The result shows that the hiding capacity and key length test is better than other techniques. Steganalysis test result is also better than other tested techniques. Furthermore, in the speed test and endurance test, the results are quite satisfactory, not far from the others technique that is tested. The proposed method had a better performance than any other method that get tested, especially in speed performance and endurance performance.
Judul Paper: Analysis and Implementation of Steganography Using Playfair Techniques and DNA Substitution To Improve Message Security
Keyword: Cryptography DNA, Steganography DNA, Data Hiding, Playfair and substitution DNA
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) affects the academic performance of youngsters. Children with ADHD struggle to remain focused during learning due to decreased attention and concentration. They are highly active and have trouble remembering teachers' instructions. Attention difficulties, focus disorders, and hyperactivity might hinder learning. This work aims to observe the impact of serious games with a platformer genre and puzzles titled "Tanji Adventure to the Diamond Temple" on the learning activities of kids with ADHD. The goal is to create a fun and engaging learning environment to boost the motivation and focus of those with ADHD. Game development process utilizes iterative prototyping. Each iteration yields a prototype that refines in the next iteration. The game was tested on children with ADHD by examining their behavior before and after playing to evaluate whether new game mechanics were necessary. The review procedure includes observing youngsters and interviewing teachers and involves specialists to evaluate its contents. The study confirms that the concentration of children with ADHD increase after playing the game. The game incorporates elements that help youngsters with ADHD concentrate and increase their attention span
Judul Paper: Serious Game to Training Focus for Children with Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder: “Tanji Adventure to the Diamond Temple”
Keyword: ADHD; focus; educational video games; children
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: Literature review is the first step in starting research for a deep understanding of the research interest. However, finding literature relevant to research interests is difficult and takes time. Skyline query is a method that can be used for filtering. An object p is said to dominate object q if p equals q on all of its attributes, and p is at least better than q on one attribute. Categorical Data Skyline Search (CDSS) is an algorithm that can filter skyline objects in categorical data types such as documents. CDSS uses Extended Distance Wu and Palmer (DEWP) to calculate the distance between the user query and document keywords. The document keywords and user queries are represented as nodes in the ACM CCS ontology, and documents are assumed to be represented by a single keyword. This study aims to use the CDSS algorithm to search for skyline documents represented by more than one keyword by adding an aggregate function (average, minimum, maximum) to the CDSS algorithm, especially in calculating DEWP. This study used the thesis documents from the IPB University computer science department. Document keywords will be extracted using the Term Frequency-Inverse Term Frequency (TF-IDF) method. The collected keywords will be mapped in a mixed ontology tree that refers to the Association of Computing Machinery Computing Classification System 2012 (ACM CCS 2012) and Computer Science Ontology (CSO) as ontology standards in computer science. The skyline query algorithm for determining skyline documents is Block Nested Loop (BNL). The evaluation method uses the skyline ratio of each aggregate function in the CDSS. Based on the ratio value, CDSS using the maximum DEWP has the most relevant skyline results compared to the average DEWP and minimum DEWP.
Judul Paper: Aggregate Functions in Categorical Data Skyline Search (CDSS) for Multi-keyword Document Search
Keyword: categorical data skyline search, aggregate function, ontology, skyline query, term frequency inverse term frequency
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: In the modern era of dynamic stock trading, the use of the Support Vector Machine Regression algorithm has become a major concern for investors and traders. This study aims to analyze how the algorithm works in predicting stock market patterns using Support Vector Machine Regression. The research method used is Support Vector Regression with a Radial Basis Function kernel. The results of the study show a good level of accuracy, with a Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of 6.04%. This indicates that this model is effective in predicting stock price movements on the Indonesia Stock Exchange. In contrast, the use of Linear and Polynomial kernels did not provide satisfactory results, with quite significant error rates of 16.32% and 22.47%, respectively. In fact, the Sigmoid kernel showed a very high error rate, namely a MAPE of 808.46%, which indicates that this model is not suitable for stock price prediction. This study contributes by demonstrating that the use of Support Vector Regression with a Radial Basis Function kernel can provide accurate prediction results in the context of stock price movements. The main contribution lies in further understanding the effectiveness of the Support Vector Regression model in prediction in the Indonesian stock market, which provides significant benefits for investors, financial companies, the government, and the public.
Judul Paper: Predicting Financial Patterns on the Indonesian Stock Exchange Stock Market Using the Support Vector Machine For Regression (SVR) Algorithm
Keyword: Stock Market; Vector Machine Regression
Jurnal: Data Science: Journal of Computing and Applied Informatics

Abstrak: Coffee drinking has become commonplace in Indonesia, especially in Pontianak, leading many entrepreneurs to establish coffee shops. Coffee shops are establishments with a more modern design than traditional coffee shops. Modern design emphasizes a more modern and comfortable atmosphere, amenities, menu, presentation, and service. The large number of coffee shops makes users hesitate to visit new coffee shops because the coffee shops they visit do not match their priorities, resulting in a lack of information about coffee shops with more complete facilities, menus, cheaper prices, longer operating hours, and accurate locations. Another problem is that many consumers still do not know the coffee shop locations in certain sub-districts and there are still some coffee shops that are not listed on maps. Thus, these problems make researchers have the goal of building a coffee shop selection system that makes it easier for consumers to determine coffee shops that match their shared priorities. Technique For Others Preference By Similarity To Ideal Solution (TOPSIS) method that makes facilities, menus, prices, and operating hours as criteria in the calculation method in determining the best coffee shop and displays location points with a library leaflet. Priorities are criteria obtained from consumer problems and then conducting observations and questionnaires as a benchmark for determining sub-criteria to carry out the decision-making process. This study conducted UAT testing on respondents to assess the feasibility of the system in admin and user sessions calculated on a Likert scale resulting in a score between 80% - 100% = Very (Agree / Good / Like) or "Very Good".
Judul Paper: Technique For Others Preference By Similarity To Ideal Solution (TOPSIS) untuk Pemilihan Coffee Shop dan Mapping Menggunakan Library Leaflet
Keyword: Decision Support Systems; Coffee Shop; TOPSIS Method; Mapping; Library Leaflets; UAT Testing
Jurnal: JEPIN (Jurnal Edukasi dan Penelitian Informatika)

Abstrak: Phishing link detection is a critical challenge in cybersecurity that requires advanced analytical techniques to distinguish between legitimate and malicious links. This is necessary because as technology advances, phishing threats are becoming increasingly complex and difficult to recognize, causing not only financial losses but also damaging an organization's reputation and further vulnerabilities to other cyberattacks. With the increasing complexity of phishing attacks, conventional approaches are no longer effective; therefore, more adaptive techniques such as machine learning are needed to recognize patterns in links that indicate potential threats. This study aims to detect phishing links early using machine learning algorithms by analyzing the effect of using feature engineering by comparing the performance of bagging-based and boosting-based algorithms. In this study, we developed new features ('Count_/_Path' and 'path_length') extracted from existing features and evaluated them using Mutual Information calculation to improve model accuracy. The results showed that the addition of 'Count_/_Path' and 'path_length' features significantly improved model performance. We also compared three machine learning algorithms: Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and XGBoost. The XGBoost algorithm with additional features performed best, with 92% accuracy, 94% recall, and 91% precision. Random Forest only achieved 91% accuracy, 92% recall, and 90% precision, while Gradient Boosting only achieved 90% accuracy, 93% recall, and 88% precision.
Judul Paper: Analysis of the Effect of Adding Features with a Comparison of Bagging and Boosting Based Algorithms on Phishing Link Detection
Keyword: Network Security; Machine Learning, Feature Engineering, Algoritma
Jurnal: Journal of Applied Informatics and Computing (JAIC)

Abstrak: Online Judge has become a commonly used platform for testing and grading in programming courses in academic environments. However, at Tanjungpura University, the grading process is still carried out manually by lecturers, resulting in complexity and potential errors. The idea of ​​implementing an online judge emerged to solve the previous problem, but the subsequent problem in the form of an external online judge has not been able to meet some specific needs of institutions or individuals. In order to overcome this challenge, this research focuses on the development of a Microservice Grader on the Online Judge of the Informatics Department, Faculty of Engineering, Tanjungpura University, which can be customized according to needs based on Event-Driven Architecture. The research methodology used, namely problem identification, literature study, data collection, system design, and test design are based on the principles of Object-Oriented Analysis and Design (OOAD). The results obtained are a Microservice Grader that can handle various cases with different feedback. The results of black box testing found 100% success from the prepared tests, which amounted to 38 test cases, proving that the system is able to handle various possible errors that may occur during use. Furthermore, a performance comparison between the platforms revealed significant variation, without detailing the influencing factors. Overall, Microservice Grader promises to contribute significantly to the development of the Online Judge system in the Informatics Department, Faculty of Engineering, Tanjungpura University, with the potential to improve assessment efficiency and accuracy.
Judul Paper: Pengembangan Microservice Grader pada Online Judge Berbasis Event-Driven Architecture
Keyword: Microservice; Online Judge; Event-Driven Architecture (EDA); Object-Oriented Analysis and Design (OOAD); Pengujian Black Box
Jurnal: JEPIN (Jurnal Edukasi dan Penelitian Informatika)

Abstrak: The Sleman Regency Government, since May 15, 2016, has launched an application called 'Lapor Sleman', which serves as a platform for submitting public complaints. Residents input complaint data into the 'Lapor Sleman' application, including complaint category, report title, details, as well as photos and coordinates. Over time, the use of social media such as Twitter has provided the public with various options for expressing their complaints. Manual classification of many Twitter posts by humans has become a routine task that should be done automatically by machines. In addition, facilitating the public in submitting complaints, including automatic complaint classification, is something that needs to be implemented. The main objective of this study is to evaluate the effectiveness and accuracy of Machine Learning (Naive Bayes Classifier) ​​and Deep Learning (Gated Recurrent Unit) methods in categorizing public complaints in Sleman Regency. The specific research objectives include: (1) Analyzing the impact of Naive Bayes Classifier (NBC) hyperparameters on accuracy during training and implementation of pickle files; (2) Analyzing GRU hyperparameters on accuracy during training and use of h5 and pth files; (3) Comparing the performance between NBC and GRU methods. The training dataset consists of 5,308 public complaints collected from the 'Lapor Sleman' application. The results show the superiority of NBC. However, NBC is slightly less in achieving overall dataset accuracy compared to PyTorch's GRU. In contrast, GRU in TensorFlow shows relatively poor performance, ranking third in most aspects. In addition, GRU in TensorFlow shows signs of overfitting, seen through a decrease in accuracy during testing with the full dataset.
Judul Paper: Comparison of Naive Bayes and Gated Recurrent Unit for Classification of Public Complaints in Sleman Regency
Keyword: machine learning, deep learning, Naive Bayes, Gated Recurrent Unit, klasifikasi teks
Jurnal: Matrik : Jurnal Manajemen, Teknik Informatika, dan Rekayasa Komputer

Abstrak: Pontianak City, located on the equator, is one of the regions in Indonesia where hydrometeorological disasters frequently occur. With its flat topography and lowland terrain, with an elevation ranging from 0.8 to 1.5 meters above sea level, it has a significant impact on disasters, one of which is tidal flooding. The overflow of the Kapuas River due to the maximum tide influenced by the Asian monsoon winds causes tidal flooding. This event poses a high risk to communities along the banks of the Kapuas River. Steps to mitigate disasters and provide information related to tidal flooding to the public need to be taken, one of which is through an approach by implementing a prediction system using machine learning technology. The system was designed using historical data obtained from the Pontianak Maritime Meteorology Station from 2017 to 2023 and processed using support vector machine, random forest, and k-nearest neighbor algorithms. The random forest algorithm has the most optimal performance in building a model with an accuracy value of 0.99 and a computation time of 1.84 seconds in the 70:30 test scenario. The implementation of the model built with the random forest algorithm is visualized on a website using the Streamlit framework. The system can classify flood occurrence levels and predict water levels according to predetermined categories. Testing was conducted during a tidal flood on December 23, 2022, at 5:00 a.m. WIB (Western Indonesian Time), showing a water level of 34 cm above ground level. The obtained MAE values ​​of 3.24, RMSE 4.86, and MAPE 1.52% indicate that the system has excellent model accuracy and can be used optimally.
Judul Paper: Pontianak City Robust Flood Prediction System Based on Machine Learning Using the Streamlit Framework
Keyword: banjir rob, ketinggian air, machine learning, random forest, streamlit
Jurnal: JITK (Jurnal Ilmu Pengetahuan dan Teknologi Komputer)

Abstrak: 2024 is a political year for the Indonesian people, where they will exercise their right to vote to determine their government leaders for the next five years. In this context, political education is crucial, especially for citizens who lack a thorough understanding of politics and the general election process. Recognizing the importance of this understanding, a group of academics created a film entitled "Dirty Vote" with the aim of raising public awareness of the election process and minimizing potential violations. This study aims to evaluate public opinion regarding the film "Dirty Vote" using two classification models, namely Naive Bayes and Logistic Regression. This study involved several stages, starting from data collection through scraping comments from the YouTube platform, data preprocessing, exploratory data analysis, to testing model performance using the K-fold Cross Validation technique, and data visualization using Word Cloud. In this study, 8,888 comment data were analyzed using natural language processing techniques to measure public sentiment towards the film. The analysis results show that the Naive Bayes algorithm identified 91.30% of positive sentiments and 8.70% of negative sentiments, while the Logistic Regression algorithm provided higher results, with positive sentiments of 95.65% and negative sentiments of 4.35%. In terms of performance, Logistic Regression proved superior with an accuracy of 95.5%, while Naive Bayes had an accuracy of 91.1%. Performance testing was carried out through one full test and eight tests under various data conditions, with performance evaluation using ROC and AUC. The results of this study indicate that both algorithms provide positive evaluations of the film "Dirty Vote", with Logistic Regression providing more accurate results.
Judul Paper: Exploration of Public Sentiment towards the Film 'Dirty Vote' using Naïve Bayes and Logistic Regression Methods
Keyword: Dirty Vote; Naïve Bayes; Logistic Regression; Sentimen Analisis; Pemilu; Politik
Jurnal: Data Science: Journal of Computing and Applied Informatics

Abstrak: The fulfillment of the SDGs in Indonesia is supported by the agricultural sector. Rice is a key agricultural product. Rice thrives in Indonesia because the soil contains nutrients. The essential nutrients required for rapid growth are nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium (NPK). Determining the NPK content in fertilizers or soil requires laboratory analysis, which is relatively expensive and time-consuming. Therefore, an alternative method is needed, namely the use of integrated soil sensors. This study aims to obtain information related to nutrient content in rice fields using integrated soil sensors and location using a geographic information system. Parameters used include soil temperature, soil moisture, electrical conductivity, soil pH, nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, and soil salinity. Other parameters obtained from GPS are longitude, latitude, velocity, altitude, and the measurement date. The stages in the research include literature review, data collection, needs analysis, design, implementation, and prototype testing. The results of this study showed that the prototype successfully identified soil nutrient content, read all parameters, and sent the results to Node-RED using MQTT, then saved the data to MySQL. Another parameter that was also stored was the location point from data collection using GPS. The conclusion of this study is that the prototype was successfully tested on rice fields and identified nutrient content. The location points sent to the database were successfully mapped using Leaflet JS.
Judul Paper: Identification of Nutrient Elements in Rice Agricultural Land using Soil Integrated Sensors and Geographic Information Systems
Keyword: NPK; Pertanian; IoT; MQTT; Soil Sensor
Jurnal: International Journal of Computer and Information System (IJCIS)

Abstrak: Tires are a component of a vehicle that plays an important role in comfort and safety aspects. Standardization is needed to ensure that the product meets the requirements in accordance with the applicable SNI. Tire products are included in the Mandatory SNI so that manufacturers must meet the requirements in order to market their products. The Product Certification Institute (LSPro) is an institution that carries out product certification activities according to the scope accredited by KAN and obtained appointments from the relevant ministries. One of them is the LSPro Kuat Struktur which is authorized to certify tire products. In its implementation, it was found that administrative activities were still carried out manually and there was no data integration which caused the delivery of information to stakeholders to be less than optimal. Therefore, an information system is needed so that organizational performance becomes effective and efficient. A good information system requires architectural design to facilitate the management of data and information. This study aims to carry out architectural design using The Open Group Architecture Framework Architecture Development Method (TOGAF ADM) which provides steps to develop Architecture Vision, Business Architecture, Information System Architecture and Technology Architecture. The resulting architectural design will be evaluated using the Traceability Matrix, then tested by making a prototype. This research resulted in an architectural design as the basis for developing a web-based information system to support SNI certification services for tire products. Test results showed that the resulting design was traceable from start to finish using the Traceability Matrix, and the prototype functioned as intended when implemented.
Judul Paper: Architectural Design of SNI Compliance Information System for Tire Products at LSPro using TOGAF ADM
Keyword: Tire; LSPRO; Information Systems Architecture Design; SNI; TOGAF
Jurnal: Jurnal Computer Science and Information Technology (CoSciTech)

Abstrak: Flooding is a natural disaster that often occurs in various regions in Indonesia, including Pontianak City. In order to minimize losses due to flooding, a flood potential forecasting information system is needed and can be disseminated to interested parties, especially local governments and the community. The purpose of this research is to design an application called the FFWS (flood forecasting and warning) application, to provide information in the form of forecasting and early warning of inundation and flooding in the Pontianak City area. This research uses two approaches, namely the data collection method to collect data in the form of daily weather, rainfall, sea tides, water levels, and historical locations of inundation/flooding in Pontianak City. The second approach is software development that focuses on creating an Android-based FFWS application that can predict areas potentially inundated by high tides and rain. The results of this study indicate that the FFWS Application presents data in spatial form, and is able to provide early warning information on the potential for flooding, with static input (topographic maps and flood hazard maps), dynamic input based on water level forecasting data (tides), and rainfall intensity. A flood zone output map was then created with specific boundaries to identify areas prone to flooding. These results are expected to be useful for stakeholders involved in managing potential flooding in Pontianak City.
Judul Paper: Android-Based Flood Forecasting and Warning System Application Design for Pontianak City
Keyword: flood; puddle; flood forecasting; early warning; FFWS application
Jurnal: Sinkron : Jurnal dan Penelitian Teknik Informatika

Abstrak: Medicinal plants are plants that have properties that can be used as medicines to cure or prevent various diseases. The use of medicinal plants in Indonesia has been very common among people since ancient times. Knowledge of medicinal plants has also been passed down from ancestors. Medicinal plants have leaves that are almost similar from one plant to another, especially in terms of leaf morphology. This leads some people to make mistakes in identifying medicinal plant leaves. In recent decades, deep learning has become a popular method for identifying objects. Deep learning has the ability to accurately identify objects, making it very suitable for identifying medicinal plant leaves. In this study, the transfer learning method was used to identify medicinal plants. Transfer learning uses a pre-trained model, so it can be used with less data and has a relatively faster computation time. The pretrained model used in this study was MobileNetV2. In this study, a fine-tuning technique was applied to improve model performance. Several experiments were conducted with different parameters such as epochs and fine-tuning layers to achieve the best results. The results of this study obtained an accuracy of 99% for training, 98% for validation, and 94% for testing.
Judul Paper: Implementation of Deep Learning for Identification of Medicinal Plant Leaves Using the Transfer Learning Method
Keyword: Tanaman Obat; Deep learning; Transfer learning; MobileNetV2; Fine tune
Jurnal: JOIV : International Journal on Informatics Visualization

Abstrak: This research is motivated by the need for an application that can evaluate the level of mastery of student practice. The purpose of the research is to produce an application for the needs of evaluating the level of mastery of student practice, which can be operated without having to run the main editor application first. The research method used is development research with 4D stages. The limitation of the research problem lies in the development of the application only for processing the practical scores of grade XI students majoring in Electrical Avionics in the Aircraft Electrical subject. Based on the development carried out, the application can be run without having to run the main editor application first, is able to process input and produce output, and can print screenshots of the application. Through processing data on student practice values, the results obtained show that the level of mastery of student practice using the application (TPF) is 75% - 80%. In contrast, the level of mastery of student practice using the manual method (TPM) is 80% to 87%. Although there are differences in results between the two methods, the relationship between TPF and TPM is strong. The Spearman correlation test shows that the correlation value between TPF and TPM is 0.609 (60.9%) and is categorized as "high". This demonstrates that the developed application is capable of determining students' level of practicum mastery. However, to determine whether the application is suitable for use on a small or large scale, further research and development are needed, such as expert validation and user acceptance testing. It is hoped that in the future, the application will be ready for widespread distribution and utilized by various schools with Electrical Avionics majors to evaluate their students' practical skills. Evaluation has a significant impact on improving the quality and standard of learning.
Judul Paper: Development of an Application for Evaluation of the Mastery Level of Aircraft Electrical Practicum Based on the Fuzzy Expert System Stand Alone Application
Keyword: Logika Fuzzy; Mamdani; Fuzzy Expert System; Aircraft Electrical; Electrical Avionics; Stand Alone Application
Jurnal: International Journal on Advanced Science, Engineering and Information Technology (IJASEIT)

Abstrak: Of the many cultures in Indonesia, each must have different languages. In a regional dialect, some have their own letters as a type of composition or depiction of the language. One of the languages ​​that has its own letters as a type of composition is the Toba Batak language, which is also called the Toba Batak script. This study aims to train a computer to recognize the characters of the Toba Batak script. In this study, the Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) method was used to recognize the handwriting of the Toba Batak script. The results of the study obtained a CNN model with a kernel size of 3x3, hidden layer 1, an input image size of 150x150 pixels, using an epoch limit of 300 epochs and from this model the accuracy in test I was 89.47%, accuracy in test II was 73.68%, accuracy in test III was 57.89%, accuracy in test IV was 84.21% and accuracy in test V was 84.21%.
Judul Paper: Handwriting Recognition of Batak Toba Script Characters using the Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) Method
Keyword: Introduction; Handwriting; Toba Batak script; Convolution Neural Network; Deep Learning
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: Spices are one of Indonesia's riches. According to data from the Negari Rempah Foundation, there are approximately 400 to 500 spice species worldwide, with 275 found in Southeast Asia, particularly Indonesia. Spices are diverse and often have similarities, making them difficult to distinguish. Therefore, to preserve knowledge about Indonesian spices, an accurate spice classification application is needed to maintain public knowledge of spices. Furthermore, in the industrial sector, it can improve efficiency in the spice industry. The use of technology in spice classification can improve efficiency in the spice industry. With the right technology, the time required to identify spice types can be accelerated and the risk of human error minimized. Limited spice imagery is also a problem in spice classification. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) with transfer learning architectures are image classification methods that perform well on limited datasets. Experiments were conducted using six CNN architectures: Xception, MobileNetV2, DenseNet201, VGG16, VGG19, and ResNet50. There are 10 types of spices classified: ginger, turmeric, key, fennel, pepper, galangal, cumin, galangal, temulawak, and coriander. Based on the experimental results, Xception was the best architecture with an F1 score of 96.99%.
Judul Paper: Classification of Spice Types Using Convolutional Neural Network and Transfer Learning
Keyword: CNN; Rempah; Deep Learning; Transfer Learning
Jurnal: JOIV : International Journal on Informatics Visualization

Abstrak: Student organizations (Ormawa) are a forum for fostering and developing student interests and talents in the arts, sports, and scientific fields. Hayam Wuruk University (UHW) Perbanas, as one of the private higher education institutions in Surabaya, has 24 Student Activity Units (UKM). All of these UKMs are incorporated into the Ormawa forum. The management of Ormawa in its development requires governance. Its implementation is through routine and specific audits to ensure the implementation of work programs and funding in accordance with quality standards and procedures. Based on this, the purpose of this study is to design a website-based internal audit information system with a waterfall model. The programming language uses HTML 5, PHP 7 and is integrated with the Ormawasite application. The system has been tested using the black-box method and the system has successfully saved, updated, and deleted the initial and final audit periods, audit work papers, and checklist assessment rubrics for audit results. The Ormawa audit system, named SIRAM, can be implemented in the next audit period as an effective and efficient implementation of higher education internal control.
Judul Paper: Student Organization Management Control System (Ormawa) in Higher Education with Website Applications
Keyword: Ormawa; Governance; Auditing; FLOWER
Jurnal: Jurnal Ilmiah Informatika Komputer

Abstrak: The rapid development of technology has provided many benefits to society in various fields. One form of utilization of this technology is the use of a Learning Management System (LMS), a software that supports online teaching and learning activities. To be comfortable to use, an LMS needs to be designed with usability aspects in mind according to the background of its potential users. Therefore, this study aims to investigate differences in perceptions of the level of LMS usability based on the background of its users, namely: gender, year of entry into college, and frequency of LMS use. This study applies a quantitative method. The research design used is survey research with data collection techniques using questionnaires. The instrument used is Usability Heuristics Evaluation. Data collection was conducted at two state universities (PT) in Yogyakarta, each conducted in two stages. Data analysis in this study used descriptive statistical analysis. The results obtained in this study are in general: i) females tend to give higher ratings of the level of LMS usability than males; ii) younger students tend to give higher ratings of the level of LMS usability than their seniors; iii) users who use the LMS more frequently tend to give a higher rating of the LMS's usability level than those who use it less frequently.
Judul Paper: Differences in Usability Heuristics Evaluation Levels in Learning Management Systems Based on User Background
Keyword: Learning Management System, Usability, Perguruan Tinggi, E-learning, 10 usability heuristics
Jurnal: JOIV : International Journal on Informatics Visualization

Abstrak: In the C4.5 decision tree method, the attribute split process is still unable to maximize the accuracy performance of the decision tree due to noise in less relevant attributes. This has implications for the size of the decision tree becoming over-fitting so that the test accuracy is less than optimal. Attribute reduction is one way that can be done in selecting data attributes that have a relatively small percentage of influence so that it is expected to be able to improve the accuracy of the data classification method. The method proposed in this study is used to reduce less relevant attributes from the dataset, namely the Chi Square method, resulting in attributes that have a large influence on the data and then classified using the C4.5 decision tree. To test the proposed model, this study uses a dataset from kaggle.com, namely South Germany Credit, which consists of 1000 data records with 20 attributes. The evaluation of the proposed classification performance is based on the Confusion Matrix. From the test results of the proposed method, it was concluded that the proposed method is able to improve the accuracy of the C4.5 decision tree with an average accuracy increase of 2.5%.
Judul Paper: Attribute Reduction Using Chi Square to Optimize the Performance of the C4.5 Decision Tree Method
Keyword: Decision Tree C4.5; Chi Square; Reduksi Atribut; Confusion Matrix
Jurnal: JIKO (Jurnal Informatika dan Komputer)

Abstrak: Ransomware is the most frightening malware threat today due to its data encryption capabilities. Furthermore, the increasing number of ransomware attacks results in significant losses. Handling these attacks is increasingly difficult due to the ever-evolving number of ransomware variants. A system capable of detecting even the newest ransomware variants is needed. In this research, we created a system capable of detecting ransomware and normalware using machine learning methods, utilizing API call data from ransomware and normalware. In this study, we only performed binary classification for all detected ransomware variants. The feature extraction process was carried out using the N-gram and TF-IDF methods on API calls to form a subset of features used in the model learning process. The detection model was built by training on API call data from several ransomware variants. Model testing was carried out on both pre-trained ransomware variants and ransomware variants outside the training data. The model learning process was carried out to find similarities in features from API call data from various ransomware variants in the training data. These feature similarities will be used to detect other ransomware variants outside the training data. The results showed that the average accuracy of the model against ransomware variants in the training data was 94% with a maximum error rate of 10%. Ransomware detection results for variants outside the training data showed an average accuracy of 83%, with a maximum error rate of 30%. Therefore, the model developed in this study can be used to detect ransomware even though ransomware variants are evolving.
Judul Paper: Ransomware Malware Detection Based on API Calls with N-gram and TF-IDF Feature Extraction Methods
Keyword: Ransomware; Panggilan API; Machine Learning; Binary Classification; N-gram; TF-IDF
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: The high mortality rate of young fry during cultivation is due to a lack of attention to the media and water quality. Fish cultivation failure can be caused by water with an acidity (pH) that is too acidic or alkaline, and inappropriate temperatures will affect mortality rates. The ideal water acidity (pH) level for catfish is 6.5–8.5. A pH level of 9 or above will decrease the catfish's appetite, and a pH level below 5 will cause mucus to clump in the catfish's gills and facilitate the growth of fungi or pathogenic bacteria. The ideal water temperature for fish growth is between 20–30°C. Sudden extreme weather changes, continuous rain, and then high heat will drastically change the water temperature and pH, causing stress and death to the catfish. Based on these observations, a tool is needed to maintain water quality, specifically temperature and pH. A Water Quality Control System, which uses pH and water temperature with proportional control, can control the acidity, pH, and water temperature in catfish cultivation. This system has proven successful in increasing growth in pond A with the system. catfish growth from 4 cm to 5-6 cm increased 7.7% higher than pond B without a system. Catfish growth to 7-8 cm in pond A was 3.5% higher than pond B. Catfish growth to 9 cm in pond A was 4% higher and mortality in pond A with a system was 16% lower compared to pond B without a system. This water quality control system in the form of pH and water temperature has been proven to be able to increase growth and suppress catfish mortality by maintaining stable temperature and pH of pond water at the setpoint value and the android application created successfully displays the value of the readings from the pH and temperature sensors that have been stored in the firebase cloud so that monitoring water quality in the form of temperature and pH of water can be done remotely.
Judul Paper: Water Quality Proportional Control System in the form of Ph and Temperature in IoT-Based Catfish Cultivation
Keyword: IoT; Proportional; Water Quality; Catfish Cultivation
Jurnal: JITK (Jurnal Ilmu Pengetahuan dan Teknologi Komputer)

Abstrak: This study explores the importance of project information monitoring applications in supporting effective decision-making in a company. The focus of this study is PT Telkom Akses Witel Semarang. Currently, internal reports are still prepared manually, causing imperfect data integration and difficulties in monitoring work invoices. The study proposes a web-based "Project Information Monitoring" application with the ICONIX Process methodology to address this problem. The goal is to improve operational ease, monitor work invoices, and centrally integrate divisional reports. The use of ICONIX Process and web technology is expected to produce a structured and documented system that meets the company's needs. This application was developed using the Laravel 10 framework with a Model View Controller (MVC) approach, aiming to ensure good and correct development. Testing of the project information monitoring application was conducted using black box testing with various scenarios based on predefined use cases. The test results showed the application's suitability to user needs in the form of acceptance in each test scenario.
Judul Paper: Development of a Web-Based Project Information Monitoring Application Using the ICONIX Process Method (Case Study: PT Telkom Access Witel Semarang)
Keyword: Monitoring Applications, Information Systems, ICONIX
Jurnal: Format : Jurnal Imiah Teknik Informatika

Abstrak: In the era of information technology advancement, maintaining data security and privacy is a very important defense. This study aims to prove Cryptographic security techniques with Homomorphic methods that can process data when the data is encrypted highlighting the shortcomings in the implementation of encryption standards that only encrypt data. This research method includes an analysis of Homomorphic Cryptography, by building an e-voting system to implement the Homomorphic method with the Pailier algorithm that can process voting data when the data is encrypted, creating keys that can Encrypt voting data and Decrypt voting data. The results of this study are the application of Cryptography with the Homomorphic method in the e-voting website system obtained successful data processing when the data is encrypted then from the results of security testing attacks using the Cipher text only attack, plaintext data could not be found and the performance time test obtained results of 0.010 seconds for the encryption decryption process, as well as the Shannon entropy test obtained results of 3.20 bits from 1 encrypted vote data
Judul Paper: Homomorphic Cryptography in Data Anonymization for Data Processing in E-Voting Systems
Keyword: cryptography, homomorphic, pailiier, encryption, decryption, e-voting
Jurnal: Jurnal ELTIKOM : Jurnal Teknik Elektro, Teknologi Informasi dan Komputer

Abstrak: This study aims to develop an educational game-based learning media application that introduces Madurese medicinal plants and herbs to 10th-grade students majoring in Pharmacy at SMKS Yannas Husada Bangkalan. This application is expected to improve students' understanding of the material on simple drugs, herbs, and rhizomes in the Pharmacognosy subject, as well as foster a higher interest in learning through an interactive and technology-based approach. The application development uses the Multimedia Development Life Cycle (MDLC) model which consists of six stages, namely concept, design, material collecting, assembly, testing, and distribution. The results of trials by media experts show that this application has achieved an achievement level of 88% and is in the qualification of very suitable for use. Assessments from material experts also show an achievement level of 88.89%, indicating that this application is valid and suitable for use. In addition, tests conducted by 10th-grade students majoring in Pharmacy at SMKS Yannas Husada Bangkalan showed an achievement level of 93%, which also indicates that this application is very suitable for use. Based on these results, it can be concluded that this application is effective and valid for use as a learning medium.
Judul Paper: Development of Madurese Medicinal Plants and Herbal Educational Game-Based Learning Media Applications
Keyword: Pharmacognosy, Educational Games, MDLC, Medicinal Plants and Herbs
Jurnal: Jurnal Computer Science and Information Technology (CoSciTech)

Abstrak: In the era of information technology advancement, the Sidokerto Village Information System Website is utilized to provide up-to-date information regarding developments within the village community. To ensure the quality of the Sidokerto Village Information System Website, software testing is necessary. This aims to prevent errors and ensure that all features and functionality are functioning properly. In this testing, we used the Profile Matching method adapted from the McCall Model indicators. The primary focus was on the Product Operation aspect, which includes Correctness, Reliability, Usability, Integrity, and Efficiency. The test results showed that the website quality achieved an average score of 4.5, indicating excellent criteria. However, several improvements to the system are still needed to improve overall quality.
Judul Paper: Implementation of Profile Matching to Measure the Quality of the Sidokerto Village Information System Website Using the McCall Model
Keyword: McCall, Testing, Software, Product Operation, Website
Jurnal: Jurnal Ilmiah Informatika Komputer

Abstrak: In facing the Industrial Revolution 4.0, technologies such as the Internet of Things, Big Data, and Artificial Intelligence are key to industrial modernization. Machine Learning approaches are used to process high-dimensional multivariable data and extract hidden relationships in complex industrial environments. Machine Learning is used to classify machine failures in building a Predictive Maintenance System. This study adopts the CRISP-DM (Cross Industry Standard Process for Data Mining) cycle consisting of business understanding, data understanding, data preparation, modeling, evaluation, and deployment stages. The Predictive Maintenance Dataset, in the form of synthetic data used in this study, reflects a real industrial situation consisting of 10,000 rows of data with ten features. Machine failure types are classified into Heat Dissipation Failure, Power Failure, Overstrain Failure, and Tool Wear Failure. Exploratory Data Analysis was conducted to obtain data summaries and visualizations. The machine learning approach uses the Logistic Regression method and the model evaluation results achieved an accuracy of 96.87%, in accordance with the data success criteria. The results of the developed machine learning modeling were then implemented in a web-based Predictive Maintenance System application to facilitate machine condition monitoring and machine failure prediction by users.
Judul Paper: Machine Learning for Machine Failure Prediction in Predictive Maintenance Systems
Keyword: CRISP-DM, exploratory data analysis, kegagalan mesin, machine learning, predictive maintenance system
Jurnal: Jurnal Masyarakat Informatika

Machine Learning approaches are used to process high-dimensional multivariable data and extract hidden relationships in complex industrial environments. Machine Learning is used to classify machine failures in building a Predictive Maintenance System. Keyword: CRISP-DM, exploratory data analysis, kegagalan mesin, machine learning, predictive maintenance system
Jurnal: Jurnal Masyarakat Informatika

Abstrak: Indonesia has a railway app called Access by KAI, released by PT Kereta Api Indonesia (KAI). The public can download and review this app through the Google Play Store. Access by KAI's rating has declined since 2022, indicating that the app has not met user expectations despite being updated. Reviews on the Google Play Store platform can be analyzed to uncover important information, one of which is sentiment. This study conducted sentiment analysis on Access by KAI reviews using word embedding with the Word2Vec model for feature extraction and classical machine learning with Naive Bayes and Logistic Regression for classification algorithms. The Logistic Regression method outperformed Naive Bayes in terms of accuracy and precision with values ​​of 68.83% and 75.49%, respectively. However, the Naive Bayes method had an advantage in terms of recall with a value of 45.07%. In this study, Access by KAI reviews had a predominantly negative sentiment of 335 out of a total of 400 test data. The words "easy" and "like" are relevant as reasons for reviews with positive sentiment, while the words "application", "pay", and "ticket" are relevant as reasons for reviews with negative sentiment.
Judul Paper: Sentiment Analysis on Access by KAI Application Reviews in Indonesian Using Word-Embedding and Classical Machine Learning
Keyword: Analisis sentimen; ulasan; access by kai; word embedding; machine learning
Jurnal: Jurnal Masyarakat Informatika

Abstrak: In the rapidly developing digital era, employee registration system applications have become an unavoidable necessity for companies to ensure efficient and effective human resource management. In this context, the usability of employee management system applications is a crucial factor in ensuring users can smoothly and efficiently utilize the provided system. Usability is an important aspect in application design that is often overlooked. The success of an application is determined not only by its features and functionality, but also by the ease of use and user satisfaction in operating it. Therefore, usability evaluation is an essential step to ensure that the developed application optimally meets user needs. Usability testing and the use of questionnaires are common approaches used in evaluating the usability of an application. Thus, by conducting direct testing with users, we can identify problems that may arise during application use and obtain direct feedback from users. Meanwhile, questionnaires provide a broader view of user satisfaction and perceptions of the application used. In the context of the CV. Cupang Semarangan employee registration system application, this study aims to evaluate the application's usability using usability testing and questionnaires. In this study, the usability values ​​for the aspects of effectiveness, efficiency, ease of use, ease of learning, and satisfaction were 87.96%, 77.47%, 64.88%, 71.43%, and 68.57%, respectively.
Judul Paper: Usability Evaluation of Employee Recording System Applications Using Usability Testing and USE Questionnaire Methods
Keyword: Usability, Usability Testing, USE Questionnaire, Employee Recording System, CV. Betta Semarangan
Jurnal: TEKNIK INFORMATIKA

Abstrak: The Vigenere Cipher is one of the classic cryptographic algorithms in the world of cryptography. This research focuses on the use of the Vigenere Cipher method and its implementation in securing a text message in ASCII format. This research uses four testing methods, namely, Avalanche Effect, Character Error Rate (CER), Bit Error Rate (BER), and Entropy. The test results show that the resulting Avalanche Effect value is on average 50% or above, meaning that a good Avalanche Effect value is obtained. In addition, the resulting CER and BER are 0, meaning that no errors occurred during the encryption process. The resulting Entropy value also increases with the length of the plaintext used and is also influenced by the use of ASCII 256 in the form of letters, numbers, and symbols.
Judul Paper: Text Cryptography Based on 8 Bit Vigenere Cipher Substitution Algorithm
Keyword: Kriptografi; Vigenere Cipher; ASCII; Avalanche Effect; Entropi;
Jurnal: Khazanah Informatika: Jurnal Ilmu Komputer dan Informatika

Abstrak: The rise of e-commerce business players has made online buying and selling competition tight so that e-commerce must be able to maintain its existence. One of the main factors that encourage customers to engage in e-commerce activities is the level of user satisfaction in receiving the website towards the quality of the e-commerce website's services. This study aims to analyze the quality factors of the Shopee website that influence the level of user satisfaction using the WebQual 4.0 method and additional user satisfaction variables. The results of data processing and analysis showed that the contribution of the influence given by the WebQual 4.0 dimensions together to user satisfaction in receiving the Shopee website was 76.2%, namely the WebQual 4.0 dimensions together had a significant influence on user satisfaction, usability had a positive and significant influence on user satisfaction, information quality had a positive and significant influence on user satisfaction, and service interaction quality had a positive and significant influence on user satisfaction.
Judul Paper: Analysis of the Effect of Shopee Website Quality on User Satisfaction Based on the WebQual 4.0 Method (Case Study: Shopee User Students at Diponegoro University)
Keyword: Shopee, website quality, user satisfaction, webqual 4.0, multiple linear regression
Jurnal: JIKO (Jurnal Informatika dan Komputer)
